title_tag: Surfing in Senegal | Ngor Surfcamp Teranga
meta_description: Surf Ngor Island, Dakar: consistent waves 365 days a year, uncrowded lineups, ISA-certified coaches. West Africa’s best-kept surf secret, a short flight from Europe.
hero_subtitle: Consistent waves year-round, uncrowded lineups, warm water — Ngor Island has been calling surfers since 1964.
intro: West Africa may not be the first place you think of for waves, but it has a serious surf pedigree. On the Cabo Verde Peninsula, Dakar picks up both northern and southern hemisphere swells — making it one of the most consistent surf destinations on the planet, 365 days a year. While Morocco’s famous spots draw hundreds of surfers at a time, Ngor’s lineups remain refreshingly uncrowded.

sections.content: Hit dawn patrol and you may share a session with fewer than half a dozen surfers — an unspoken camaraderie in warm Atlantic water, just a short flight from Europe. From the legendary Ngor Right, made famous by The Endless Summer film in 1964, to the beach breaks of the southern coast, the variety of waves around Dakar suits every level and mood.
bullets: Both northern and southern hemisphere swells — waves 365 days a year
Warm water, no wetsuit needed most of the year

sections.h2: Expert Coaching, Two Sessions a Day
sections.content: Two sessions a day — morning and afternoon — at the best spot chosen by forecast and swell. Our surf guide Abu, rated the best in Dakar by our guests, knows exactly how to position you on the wave. Video analysis, free surf theory classes and personalised coaching make every session count.
bullets: ISA-certified local coaches who grew up on these reefs
Video analysis to fast-track your progression

sections.h2: For Beginners, Intermediates and Advanced Surfers
sections.content: The warm water and gentle sets at Ngor Left make learning feel natural — beginners finish their stay standing on green waves. Intermediates discover Ngor Right on smaller days, a fast hollow reef break with enough face to play with. Advanced surfers push their limits on Ngor Right at full size or venture to the powerful A-frames of Les Almadies on the southern coast.
bullets: Ngor Left: forgiving sandy bottom, perfect for building confidence
Ngor Right: the Endless Summer wave — fast, hollow and demanding

sections.content: Beyond the water, Ngor Island life takes over: no cars, no noise, just the sound of waves and the warmth of Senegalese teranga. The island empties by evening — just the glow of Dakar’s lights across the water. From monkfish brochettes at the local fish shacks to café touba and rooftop sunsets with African beats, you will grow in and out of the surf.
bullets: 5-minute pirogue ride from Dakar — a world apart
Authentic Senegalese cuisine, music and culture

cta_text: Stay at Ngor Surfcamp Teranga and ride the waves that inspired The Endless Summer. ISA-certified coaches, uncrowded lineups and the warmth of Senegalese teranga await.
og_title: Surfing in Senegal | Ngor Surfcamp Teranga
og_description: Explore warm-water surf in Senegal from Ngor Island. Discover local waves, all-level sessions and the relaxed spirit of Ngor Surfcamp Teranga.
